title: erythroxylum boinense
intro: Erythroxylum boinense, also known as Bois de Fer, is a species of plant in the Erythroxylum genus. It is native to the tropical forests of French Guiana and is a member of the Erythroxylum family, which includes the coca plant. The medicinal properties of Erythroxylum boinense include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, making it a valuable plant in traditional medicine. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to alleviate various health issues and provide general well-being.
